intro: Aporosa arborea, commonly known as the Indian copperleaf or copperleaf tree, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aporosa arborea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
